channel.title: Solar Eclipse on Clusters and Climate
channel.description: Recent content in Solar Eclipse on Clusters and Climate

item.title: How I Followed the Solar Eclipse
item.description:
2010 – The First Experience

My 2010s kicked off with a bang: the longest annular solar eclipse of the millennium, on 15 January 2010. The annular phase lasted just over 11 minutes, with the moon covering about 91% of the Sun. I joined a group of fellow eclipse chasers and traveled more than 3000 km from Pune to Rameshwaram—just to catch those magical moments.

item.title: When the Sun-Moon duo turned into the Ring of Fire
item.description: Following article is written by one of the participants of solar eclipse expedition - Sania Rahigude. She pours out her journey and experience of Solar Eclipse Expedition through this article.
